description: Companies engaged in the production of goods through physical, chemical, or mechanical transformation of materials. Includes discrete manufacturing (automotive, electronics, machinery) and process manufacturing (chemicals, food, pharmaceuticals). Mid-market manufacturers typically operate 1-3 plants with 50-500 employees and revenues of €10-100M.

processes.name: Procure-to-Pay (P2P)
processes.description: End-to-end process from identifying procurement need through purchase requisition, purchase order issuance, goods receipt, three-way matching (PO/GR/invoice), and supplier payment. Typically involves Finance, Procurement, and Warehouse departments.

processes.name: Order-to-Cash (O2C)
processes.description: Full cycle from customer order receipt through production planning, manufacturing execution, quality control, shipping, invoicing, and cash collection. Involves Sales, Production, Logistics, and Finance.

processes.name: Plan-to-Produce (P2P)
processes.description: Production planning based on demand forecasts and actual orders, including MRP (Material Requirements Planning) runs, work order creation, shop floor scheduling, BOM explosion, and capacity planning.

processes.name: Record-to-Report (R2R)
processes.description: Financial closing process including cost center accounting, cost of goods sold calculation, inventory valuation, overhead allocation, variance analysis, and period-end reporting to management and regulators.

processes.name: Hire-to-Retire (H2R)
processes.description: HR lifecycle management from workforce planning, recruitment, onboarding, payroll processing, performance management, training, and eventual separation. Critical for labor-intensive manufacturing environments.

processes.name: Maintain-to-Operate (MtO)
processes.description: Maintenance management encompassing preventive maintenance scheduling, corrective work order management, spare parts inventory, MTBF/MTTR tracking, and equipment lifecycle management (CMMS integration).

processes.name: Design-to-Release (D2R)
processes.description: Product development from concept through engineering design, prototype, testing, regulatory approval, BOM creation, routing definition, and release to production. Involves Engineering, Quality, Regulatory Affairs.

processes.name: Quality Management
processes.description: Integrated quality processes including incoming inspection (IQC), in-process quality control (IPQC), final inspection, non-conformance management (NCR), CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Actions), and customer complaint handling.

kpis.name: Purchase Order Cycle Time
kpis.unit: days from requisition to approved PO

kpis.name: On-Time Delivery (OTD)
kpis.unit: % of orders delivered on promised date

kpis.name: Three-Way Match Rate (Automatic)
kpis.unit: % of supplier invoices auto-matched without manual intervention

kpis.name: First Pass Yield (FPY)
kpis.unit: % of units passing quality inspection without rework

pain_points: Manual data entry from paper invoices and delivery notes leads to high error rates (5-15%) and slow processing times, creating bottlenecks in Accounts Payable
Lack of real-time visibility into purchase order status and goods receipt causes payment delays and strained supplier relationships
Disconnected systems between ERP, quality management, and document storage result in duplicate data entry and compliance risks
Physical document archives occupy expensive warehouse space and make audit preparation extremely time-consuming (days instead of hours)
Manual approval workflows for purchase requisitions and invoices cause delays, especially when approvers are travelling or unavailable
Inability to perform automated three-way matching (PO/GR/Invoice) forces manual reconciliation consuming 30-40% of AP team time
Non-compliance with e-invoicing mandates (e.g., KSeF in Poland, ZUGFeRD/XRechnung in Germany) creates legal and financial risk
Poor document version control in engineering and quality departments leads to use of outdated specifications on the shop floor
Difficulty tracing document history for ISO 9001 audits results in audit findings and potential certification risks
Fragmented communication between production, procurement, and finance delays month-end closing by 3-5 days
typical_systems: ERP: SAP S/4HANA, SAP Business One, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central, Epicor, Infor LN/CloudSuite
MES (Manufacturing Execution System): Siemens SIMATIC IT, Rockwell FactoryTalk, Epicor Advanced MES
Quality Management System (QMS): ETQ Reliance, MasterControl, SAP QM module
CMMS (Maintenance): IBM Maximo, SAP PM, Infor EAM, eMaint
Document Management / DMS: SharePoint, M-Files, DocuWare, Nuxeo, OpenText
WMS (Warehouse Management): SAP EWM, Manhattan Associates, Körber WMS
PLM (Product Lifecycle Management): Siemens Teamcenter, PTC Windchill, Dassault Systèmes ENOVIA
Business Intelligence: SAP Analytics Cloud, Microsoft Power BI, Qlik
regulatory_considerations: GDPR (EU) – Personal data in HR documents, customer contracts, and supplier records; requires lawful basis, data minimization, and retention schedules
ISO 9001:2015 – Quality management system documentation, controlled documents, records retention, CAPA processes
ISO 14001:2015 – Environmental management documentation for manufacturers with significant environmental aspects
E-invoicing mandates – KSeF (Poland), ZUGFeRD/XRechnung (Germany), FatturaPA (Italy), SII (Spain) for B2B and B2G transactions
Product safety regulations – CE marking documentation, RoHS/REACH compliance records, technical files
Customs and export control – EUR.1 certificates, dual-use export documentation, customs tariff records
Financial reporting – IFRS/local GAAP cost accounting, inventory valuation documentation, transfer pricing
Labor law – Working time records, health & safety documentation, collective agreements (EU Posted Workers Directive)
